questions.text: How many consecutive oaths does Allah take at the start of Surah Ash-Shams?
questions.answer: Seven oaths (Sun, Moon, Day, Night, Sky, Earth, and the Soul).

questions.text: What does 'Duhaha' mean in Verse 1?
questions.answer: The brightness or morning light of the sun.

questions.text: What does the Moon do in Verse 2?
questions.answer: It follows the sun.

questions.text: How is the 'Day' described in relation to the sun?
questions.answer: As that which reveals or displays its glory.

questions.text: How is the 'Night' described in relation to the sun?
questions.answer: As that which covers or conceals it.

questions.text: What did Allah inspire the soul with (Verse 8)?
questions.answer: Its wickedness (Fujur) and its righteousness (Taqwa).

questions.text: Who has succeeded according to Verse 9?
questions.answer: He who has purified the soul (Zakkaha).

questions.text: Who has failed according to Verse 10?
questions.answer: He who has corrupted/instilled it with sin (Dassaha).

questions.text: Which nation is used as an example of failure?
questions.answer: The nation of Thamud.

questions.text: Why did Thamud deny their Prophet?
questions.answer: Because of their 'transgression' (Taghwaha).

questions.text: Who was the 'most wretched' (Ashqaha) of Thamud?
questions.answer: The man (Qudar bin Salif) who stepped forward to kill the She-Camel.

questions.text: What was the command of the Messenger of Allah (Salih) regarding the She-Camel?
questions.answer: '[Do not harm] the she-camel of Allah or [prevent her from] her drink'.

questions.text: What did the people of Thamud do to the camel?
questions.answer: They denied him (the Prophet) and hamstrung/killed her.

questions.text: How did Allah punish Thamud?
questions.answer: He crushed them for their sin and leveled them.

questions.text: Does Allah fear the 'consequence' of His punishment?
questions.answer: No, 'He does not fear its consequence' (Verse 15).

questions.text: What is the spiritual significance of the sun/moon contrast in this Surah?
questions.answer: It reflects the dual nature of the human soul (good and evil) and the clarity of guidance.

questions.text: What does 'Tahlaha' mean in Verse 6?
questions.answer: How Allah spread or expanded the earth.

questions.text: Is the ability to choose between good and evil innate to humans?
questions.answer: Yes, Verse 8 indicates Allah inspired the soul with the knowledge of both.

questions.text: What is the 'She-Camel of Allah'?
questions.answer: A miraculous sign sent to Thamud that was directly attributed to Allah's power.

questions.text: What is the main takeaway regarding the soul?
questions.answer: Success is not accidental; it is the result of active purification (Tazkiyah).
